successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.interface.ts exports ILeaveManagementService with getLeaveBalance and getLeaveHistory methods added.

src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.ts injects IAuditService and enforces RBAC: approve/reject only allowed for users with role 'manager' who are the manager of the requesting employee; cancel allowed for owning employee or their manager; discard draft allowed only for owning employee when status is 'draft'.

src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.ts getLeaveBalance returns only the authenticated employee's balances when role is 'employee', and any employee's balances when role is 'manager' and the employee is in their team.

src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.ts getLeaveHistory returns only the authenticated employee's requests when role is 'employee', and all team requests when role is 'manager' and the employee is the manager of the requesters.

src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.ts all state-changing methods (approve, reject, cancel, submit) write an audit record via IAuditService.log with correct entity type, entity id, changed by, old and new values.

src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.ts approveLeaveRequest and rejectLeaveRequest execute the status update and audit log insertion within a single database transaction to ensure atomicity.

src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.test.ts includes Jest tests that verify RBAC enforcement: a non-manager user calling approve/reject receives an error; a manager not managing the employee receives an error; an employee calling cancel on another's request receives an error.
src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.test.ts includes Jest tests that verify getLeaveBalance and getLeaveHistory return correct data scoped to the user's role.
src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.test.ts includes Jest tests that verify audit logging is called with correct parameters for each state-changing operation.

constraints: Repository pattern: All database access goes through repository interfaces.
Audit records: All state-changing operations write an audit record.
Input validation: Validate all inputs at API boundaries before processing.
No sensitive data in logs: Never log passwords, tokens, PII, or financial data.
RBAC enforcement: All API endpoints enforce role-based access control.
Error handling: No unhandled promise rejections. All async errors are caught and handled.
Approve/reject operations must be atomic with audit logging within a single database transaction.
outOfScope: Implementation of repository interfaces (ILeaveRepository, IBalanceRepository, etc.)
Integration tests beyond unit tests for the service
Other modules (leave, balance, policy, employee, notification) except as dependencies
